### Post by ciro8 on 2016-05-25
Hello,

I'm tryinh to drop all traffic in my computer with the exception of HTTP traffic, this is how the "*filters" section in my iptables.up.rules look like:
[I]
# Generated by iptables-save v1.6.0 on Wed May 25 10:58:23 2016
*filter
:FORWARD DROP
:OUTPUT DROP
:INPUT DROP
# HTTP
-A INPUT -p tcp -s 0/0 -d 192.168.1.105 --dport 80 --sport 0:65535 -m state --state NEW,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT
-A OUTPUT -p tcp -s 192.168.1.105 --sport 80 -d 0/0 --dport 0:65535 -m state --state ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT
COMMIT
# Completed on Wed May 25 10:58:23 2016[/I]



I'm specifying source and destination ip addresses and ports for when I add future rules. 

I've been checking in different sites and this seems to be the proper configuration, but I must be missing something. Could someone help on this? I've been trying different options in the last hours and can't make it work.

Regards
